summary: Backend-only regression run for PlayPal. 30/30 pytest cases passed against the public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL covering health, mocked auth (OTP request/verify, invalid-code 400, is_new toggling), users (profile/sports/history/404), matches (list>=14, sport+today filters, detail, idempotent join, create), teams (list/detail with populated roster + upcoming_matches, create), chats (user_id required, list, kind=team filter, detail with messages, text & poll send + persistence), contacts/suggested, discovery (try-new, competitions), and a global _id leak sweep across all list endpoints. No issues detected.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single ~600 line module; acceptable for MVP but consider splitting routes/seed/models into modules as it grows beyond 700 LOC.
GET /api/chats requires user_id and FastAPI returns 422 (not 400) when missing — tests accept both; if a 400 contract is desired, validate manually.
Match join allows duplicate user_id check before push but does not validate user exists; non-blocking for current scope.
verify-otp accepts ANY 6-digit code (by design, mocked) — clearly documented in test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Created transient TEST_ users via mock OTP, TEST_Match, TEST_Squad team, and TEST_hello / TEST_poll messages in the first seeded chat. No production seed data was modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_playpal_api.py — it auto-generates a unique phone per session, so it is safe to re-run. Set EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL env var (defaults to the preview URL). Frontend testing was explicitly out of scope this iteration.
